UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, DEFENDANT DAVID WALZER'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. 1ST C/A FOR CONSTRUCTIVE EVICTION- OVERRULED, PLAINTIFF ALLEGES THAT SHE WAS DEPRIVED OF "BENEFICIAL ENJOYMENT" OF THE PREMISES; 2ND AND 3RD C/A FOR IIED AND NIED- SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE FACTS AMOUNTING TO OUTRAGEOUS BEHAVIOR; 4TH C/A FOR BREACH OF WARRANTY OF HABITABILITY - SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE FACTS SHOWING THAT DEFENDANT'S ACTIONS RENDERED PREMISES RENTED TO PLAINTIFF UNINHABITABLE; 5TH AND 6TH C/A FOR NEGLIGENCE AND NUISANCE - OVERRULE, ALL ELEMENTS ARE SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGED; 7TH C/A - SUSTAIN WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO CITE ANY AUTHORITY FORT HE PROPOSITION THAT "ELIGIBLE TENANT'S" 12 MONTH PERIOD INCLUDES PERIOD AFTER SERVICE OF NOTICE. 8TH C/A FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT- SUSTAIN WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO SPECIFY PROVISIONS OF THE CONTRACT ALLEGEDLY BREACHED. PREVAILING PARTY PREPARE ORDER. JUDGE A JAMES ROBERTSON, CLERK K. LIU, NOT REPORTED.

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-01-2013 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-30-2013 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Jul-16-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-19-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT DAVID WALZER's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT.
1st c/a for constructive eviction- OVERRULED, Plaintiff alleges that she was deprived of "beneficial enjoyment" of the premises;
2nd and 3rd c/a for IIED and NIED- SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND to allege facts amounting to outrageous behavior;
4th c/a for breach of warranty of habitability - SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND to allege facts showing that Defendant's actions rendered premises rented to Plaintiff uninhabitable;
5th and 6th c/a for negligence and nuisance - OVERRULE, all elements are sufficiently alleged;
7th c/a - SUSTAIN WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. Plaintiff fails to cite any authority fort he proposition that "Eligible Tenant's" 12 month period includes period after service of Notice.
8th c/a for breach of contract- SUSTAIN WITH LEAVE TO AMEND to specify provisions of the contract allegedly breached. Prevailing party prepare order. Judge A James Robertson, Clerk K. Liu, not reported. (D501)

MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-19-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT DAVID WALZER'S MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND to allege facts supporting request for punitive damages. Prevailing party prepare order. Judge A James Robertson, Clerk K. Liu, not reported. (D501)
